title: IRGC Announces Identification and Stopping of Two Ships in Strait of Hormuz
summary: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) identified and stopped two ships in the Strait of Hormuz to enforce Iran's territorial rights.
contents:
# IRGC Announces Identification and Stopping of Two Ships in Strait of Hormuz

## Overview
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) identified and stopped two ships in the Strait of Hormuz to enforce Iran's territorial rights. This action underscores ongoing tensions in the strategic waterway. No further details on the ships' nationalities or cargo got specified in the announcement.

## Reporting Window
- Reported timeframe: 2026-04-25 (reported within past 7 days as of April 30, 2026)
- Location: Strait of Hormuz (Iran)

## Key Details
- IRGC conducted the operation to "uphold the rights of the noble Iranian nation."
- Ships got directly identified and halted by Iranian forces.
- Event aligns with flashpoint monitoring in Iran-US/Israel trigger list.
- No mention of Russian, Chinese, or specific warship/frigate involvement; focuses on unspecified vessels.
- Part of broader Strait of Hormuz security dynamics tracked by International Crisis Group.

## Source Context
The @WarshipCam account on X (Twitter) covers WarshipCam's subject area as documented in this repository's source manifest. The development above appears in news reporting that aligns with the topical scope this account monitors. Verification stems from the cited publication, not from a direct tweet capture (Twitter API access remained blocked during this collection cycle).
